title: RDX Profile Configuration
description: Defines which fields are required, optional, or not allowed in an RDX document. Profiles are consumed by tools (not by the JSON Schema validator itself) to enforce organization- or standard-specific data completeness requirements. A profile references field paths using dot notation with '*' as the array-item wildcard.

description.description: Detailed description of the profile purpose and intended use case

rdxSchemaVersion.description: The RDX schema version this profile applies to (e.g., '0.1.0')

created.description: ISO 8601 date-time when this profile was created

createdBy.description: Author or organization that created this profile

defaultFieldRequirement.description: Default requirement level applied to any RDX field not explicitly listed in fieldRequirements. Use 'optional' to allow all undeclared fields, 'required' to mandate them, or 'notAllowed' to prohibit them.

fieldRequirements.description: Map of RDX field paths to their requirement level. Paths use dot notation; '*' is a wildcard for all items in an array (e.g., 'riskSet.assets.*.properties'). A path ending at an array section (e.g., 'riskSet.attackSteps') controls whether the section itself must be present and non-empty.

integrity.description: Cryptographic hash of the profile content for tamper detection in transit. Computed over the canonical JSON serialization of the profile document with the 'integrity' field replaced by an empty object {}. Canonical form: keys sorted lexicographically, no extra whitespace, UTF-8 encoded.
